basic-string-functions.label: Basic String Functions
basic-string-functions.summary: Use functions like UPPER, LOWER, and TRIM to clean up text.

sk1.title: What Are Basic String Functions?
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
SQL provides several built-in functions to help you clean and format text data. Common functions include:

- **UPPER()**: Converts all letters in a string to uppercase.
- **LOWER()**: Converts all letters in a string to lowercase.
- **TRIM()**: Removes leading and trailing spaces from a string.

These functions are useful for making text comparisons consistent and for cleaning up messy data.

sk2.title: Why Use String Functions?
sk2.bodyMarkdown: Text data can be messy—users might enter names with extra spaces or inconsistent capitalization. Using string functions helps standardize your data, making it easier to search, compare, and analyze.

sk3.title: Examples of String Functions
sk3.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you have a table of customer names with inconsistent formatting:

```sql
SELECT name FROM customers_cleanup;
```

You can use string functions to clean up the names:

- `SELECT UPPER(name) FROM customers_cleanup;` — All names in uppercase.
- `SELECT LOWER(name) FROM customers_cleanup;` — All names in lowercase.
- `SELECT TRIM(name) FROM customers_cleanup;` — Names with no leading or trailing spaces.
